# services.mail

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.mail`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.mail.sendmailSetuidWrapper.capabilities` | `strings concatenated with ","` | A comma-separated list of capability clauses to be given to the wrapper program. The format for capability clauses is described in the “TEXTUAL REPRESENTATION” section of the {manpage}`cap_from_text(3)` manual page. For a list of capabilities supported by the system, check the {manpage}`capabilities(7)` manual page. ::: {.note} `cap_setpcap`, which is required for the wrapper program to be able to raise caps into the Ambient set is NOT raised to the Ambient set so that the real program cannot modify its own capabilities!! This may be too restrictive for cases in which the real program needs cap_setpcap but it at least leans on the side security paranoid vs. too relaxed. ::: |
| `services.mail.sendmailSetuidWrapper.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the wrapper. |
| `services.mail.sendmailSetuidWrapper.group` | `string` | The group of the wrapper program. |
| `services.mail.sendmailSetuidWrapper.owner` | `string` | The owner of the wrapper program. |
| `services.mail.sendmailSetuidWrapper.permissions` | `file mode string` | The permissions of the wrapper program. The format is that of a symbolic or numeric file mode understood by {command}`chmod`. |
| `services.mail.sendmailSetuidWrapper.program` | `null or string` | The name of the wrapper program. Defaults to the attribute name. |
| `services.mail.sendmailSetuidWrapper.setgid` | `boolean` | Whether to add the setgid bit the wrapper program. |
| `services.mail.sendmailSetuidWrapper.setuid` | `boolean` | Whether to add the setuid bit the wrapper program. |
| `services.mail.sendmailSetuidWrapper.source` | `absolute path` | The absolute path to the program to be wrapped. |
